frameworks.name: individual electric field modeling
frameworks.one_line: Person-specific head models predict intracranial E-field from tES

frameworks.name: transcranial alternating current stimulation
frameworks.one_line: α-tACS entrains and induces aftereffects on α-band oscillations

frameworks.name: MEG source localization
frameworks.one_line: Source-localized α-power links E-field to neural outcome

frameworks.name: dose-response framework
frameworks.one_line: Treats E-field intensity as the stimulation dose driving response

core_question: Can individual electric field modeling explain inter-individual variability of tACS aftereffects on α-oscillations?
key_assumptions: Individual neuroanatomy drives variability of intracranial E-fields
E-field at source locations is the causally relevant tES dose
MEG-derived α-power indexes the tACS aftereffect of interest
manipulations: α-tACS (1 mA, 20 min) vs sham stimulation
key_findings: α-tACS produced larger α-band power increase than sham
Inter-individual variability of the aftereffect was significantly predicted by individual E-field measures
E-field variability tracked variability in tACS outcomes across participants
interpretations: Variability of tACS effects is directly linked to variability in intracranial electric fields
Stimulation protocols should be individualized and analyzed as dose-response relationships
